title: The Problem Solvers | Anton Osika at Lovable
summary: Lovable is a platform that allows anyone to build software through conversation without requiring an engineering team. The product reached millions of users within two months of launch and is built with Claude as its underlying foundation. Anton Osika, the founder, emphasizes trust as a key competitive advantage achieved through craft and partnership with Anthropic.
writeup: Lovable's founder speaks at an Anthropic event about building a conversational software-creation platform aimed at non-developers. The talk covers the company's rapid user growth since launching two months prior, the deepening partnership with Anthropic (moving from a customer-vendor relationship toward a collaborative one), and the founder's thesis that trust is the most underrated moat in AI. The through-line is that eliminating friction in software creation expands human agency and enables more people to solve societal problems.
key_points: Lovable lets anyone build software through conversation with its platform, targeting people who have ideas but no engineering background.
The company launched two months before this recording and reported strong user growth, with millions of people building on the platform.
Anthropic assigned dedicated team members to work alongside Lovable, shifting the dynamic from a pure customer-vendor relationship toward a joint-building partnership.

The founder argues that a trusted brand people return to is the most underrated competitive moat in AI, and that it cannot be achieved overnight but requires obsessive attention to the customer experience.
The broader thesis is that reducing friction in starting companies and building products unlocks more human agency and leads to more societal problems being solved.
